validation_gates.reason: Repository identity, current base, target path and prior blob, UPDATE_IN_PLACE operation, review branch, two-file change budget, non-goals, and no-merge boundary were resolved before mutation.

No open pull request or matching modernization branch targeting packages/README.md surfaced. Main advanced through unrelated files, the target retained blob fc18fb3334fefe992a551fe12aa98c812232cd17, and the review branch was created from main@005aa64f6d42aa5961646e733289a2b857292357.

The existing canonical packages root README was updated in place. The generated receipt uses the established data/receipts/generated process-memory lane. No root, package lane, authority home, lifecycle phase, release path, deployment, or public route was created or promoted.

The complete v0.2 README was read; its purpose, authority, trust membrane, inputs, exclusions, package map, Mermaid relationship, child expectations, inspection path, validation, safe-change sequence, definition of done, open items, no-loss note, and status summary were preserved or strengthened.

The first twelve H2 sections exactly match Directory Rules section 15: Purpose, Authority level, Status, What belongs here, What does NOT belong here, Inputs, Outputs, Validation, Review burden, Related folders, ADRs, and Last reviewed.

Bounded exact reads and current-commit index queries established at least twenty-one direct README lanes, mixed Python/Node workspace metadata, sampled placeholder source bodies, README-only boundaries, missing package-wide tests, and partial workflows without upgrading path or manifest presence into supported capability.

The README keeps apps/packages frozen as an anomaly; preserves contracts, schemas, policy, data, release, app, pipeline, connector, tool, and runtime authority; and leaves MapLibre naming, Evidence overlap, test-home, registry-helper, and distribution conflicts visible rather than silently resolving them.

Root Node workspaces, TODO scripts, absent deterministic Node lock/pin evidence, root Hatchling scope, child Python scaffolds, partial dependency scanning, software-distribution separation, compatibility, correction, and rollback were reconciled without inventing a package manager, build, registry, release, or consumer.

The remote README is UTF-8 with LF endings and a final newline; KFM metadata parses; one real H1, sixty headings without hierarchy jumps, balanced language-tagged fences, balanced HTML containers, and alt text on all seven badge images were validated.

Nineteen custom anchors are unique, all seventeen legacy v0.2 fragments are retained, forty-three internal references resolve, and forty-eight distinct repository-relative links passed path-safety review against current-session evidence.

No private key, common token pattern, credential, private endpoint, source payload, protected coordinate, living-person or genomic record, rare-species/archaeology location, infrastructure vulnerability, or hidden reasoning was introduced.

GitHub returned blob 154e1c9a8b841397bceb52e6b4933b241906ab9a on commit 9c074db5a92968eef624a5a47360e4072e9e0210, matching the locally recomputed Git blob; the artifact SHA-256 is c08e0076eeb4f08cb786fb076a474be80f2aef85aee2b28aadea3fa9425e54f7.

The receipt was checked against schemas/contracts/v1/receipts/generated_receipt.schema.json, including required fields, artifact/hash closure, core truth label, validation outcomes, citations, pending human review, model metadata, timestamp, and no override.

Documentation/provenance-only change; no package code, manifest, dependency, lockfile, export, test, fixture, workflow, consumer, distribution, deployment, runtime, lifecycle record, release object, or public behavior changed.

Pull-request workflows run after draft PR creation. No CI success, merge readiness, human approval, package certification, distribution approval, release, or publication claim is made at receipt emission time.
Human review remains pending and separate from generation, validation, commit, merge, software distribution, KFM release, and publication.
